steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a topic addressed by both texts. Text 1 establishes that scientists still have much to learn about sloths because sloths live high in trees and are hard to access. Text 2 describes a hat-like monitor that allows scientists to finally observe sloths' hidden behaviors and correct long-held misconceptions. The shared thread is scientists and what they know (or are trying to learn) about sloths.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — How extinct sloths compare with living sloths.: Text 1 briefly mentions the extinct greater Haitian ground sloth, but Text 2 does not address extinct species at all. ✗
  • Choice B — Scientists' efforts to understand sloths.: Text 1 explains the barrier to understanding (inaccessibility), and Text 2 presents a solution (the monitor). Both texts are directly about scientists trying to understand sloths. ✓
  • Choice C — The benefits of putting monitoring devices on sloths.: only Text 2 discusses the monitor; Text 1 does not mention any device. ✗
  • Choice D — Changes in sloths' habitats.: neither text discusses habitat changes. ✗
That matches Choice B — Scientists' efforts to understand sloths..
